clinical_validity.rationale: Leadership accepts the clinical simulation direction with specialty review, communication-style QA, and human governance.

psychometric_defensibility.rationale: Trace, grounding, review, and score-use gates are credible for formative pilots, while high-stakes interpretation remains blocked.

technical_feasibility.rationale: The deterministic first milestone, WebSocket-first transport, offline assets, and provider adapters make the system buildable.

architecture_coherence.rationale: The architecture now links scenarios, actors, memory, communication profiles, assets, providers, admin UX, and trace review coherently.

security_privacy.rationale: Synthetic data and provider provenance are strong, with privacy, retention, and threat-model artifacts still required.

ux_workflow_fit.rationale: The plan defines separate examinee XR and admin/faculty UX surfaces with concrete testing and design tools.

cost_performance_efficiency.rationale: B1 is properly scoped to orchestration and heavy generation is kept offline or provider-side.

open_source_sustainability.rationale: Runtime licensing rules, asset provenance, and copyleft isolation are now explicit.

market_gtm_strength.rationale: The product story is credible as formative XR clinical skills with faculty review, case bank, and communication-skill differentiation.

evidence_discipline.rationale: The team distinguished source-supported claims from unsupported claims and captured evidence debt.

implementation_readiness.rationale: The handoff is ready for a later code implementation plan for one deterministic station.

adversarial_robustness.rationale: The red team materially improved license, provider, performance, and score-use gates.

legal_regulatory_resilience.rationale: No-equivalence, no-high-stakes, human-review, provider-review, and license-governance posture is clear.

specialty_clinical_generalizability.rationale: The case bank spans specialties and adds physician advisory roles, but each scenario family still needs review.

critical_risks.summary: Voice, ASR, and embodied animation latency could break immersion unless provider and device benchmarks pass.
Scenario-bank growth could outpace human review capacity and weaken clinical or psychometric defensibility.

evidence_debt.summary: Complete Quest 3, provider, ASR/TTS, and asset-pipeline benchmarks before implementation dependency lock.
Run clinician/psychometrician review of the 12 seed cases before labeling them pilot-ready.

decision_debt.summary: Select the exact first implementation scope: deterministic ED chest pain station with mock providers and asset placeholders.
Approve the provider benchmark matrix before testing Grok, local LLM, or other cloud providers.

summary: Senior leadership conditionally approves the technology and case-bank handoff for a later implementation plan, with runtime, license, provider, review, and score-use gates still active.
